dimensions.name: behavior_preservation
dimensions.description: Does the refactor preserve the original behavior for all valid inputs? High = semantically equivalent or explicitly flags the difference. Low = silently changes behavior, breaks edge cases, or introduces new bugs.

dimensions.name: improvement_actually_helps
dimensions.description: Is the refactor a real improvement (clarity, performance, idiomaticity), or just a stylistic shuffle? High = measurably better (shorter, faster, easier to read for the stated goal). Low = lateral move, or churn for churn's sake.

dimensions.name: scope_discipline
dimensions.description: Does the refactor stay scoped to what was asked? High = touches only the relevant code. Low = renames variables in unrelated functions, adds features, restructures layers beyond the request.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must attempt the refactor or explicitly say ALREADY_CLEAN. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: syntactically_valid
hard_gates.description: The refactored code must parse without SyntaxError in the named language.

hard_gates.name: no_silent_semantic_change
hard_gates.description: If the refactor changes behavior, it MUST be flagged in the explanation. A silently behavior-changing refactor (e.g., changing `>=` to `>`, swapping args, dropping error handling) without explicit note fails this gate.
